draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer high-contrast, digital precision for quick pace runs over soft, analog aesthetics
facade.label: Glowing blue numbers
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward tech-forward, high-legibility interfaces; likely values speed and data clarity over warmth
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer warmer color palettes, softer typography, or more natural visual metaphors for outdoor activity context

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer clean visual hierarchy with high contrast to scan info at a glance during activity
facade.axisTargeted: Light vs dark / complexity vs simplicity
facade.label: Bright, minimal cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values clarity and speed over visual richness; likely prefers minimal ornamentation, strong type contrast, and whitespace-heavy layouts
facade.rejectImplies: User may favor darker themes, more textured backgrounds, or denser information packaging; prefers visual warmth or playfulness over clinical simplicity

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond better to encouraging, conversational tone over clinical/formal language
facade.label: Cheerful coach voice
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers warm, motivational personality—app should sound like a supportive running buddy, use exclamation marks, celebrate splits/pace
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers neutral or authoritative tone—app should be matter-of-fact, data-focused, minimal personality

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer high-contrast, digital precision for quick pace runs over soft, analog aesthetics
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward tech-forward, high-legibility interfaces; likely values speed and data clarity over warmth

facade.hypothesis: Runners need quick access to core metrics (pace, distance, weather) without opening modals—a persistent left sidebar with icon-only buttons reduces friction during active runs.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation architecture: sidebar vs top tabs vs bottom floating actions
facade.label: Sidebar with icons
facade.acceptImplies: User values always-visible quick actions and spatial consistency over screen real estate; likely to accept persistent navigation patterns.
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes full-screen content and minimal UI chrome; expects navigation in tabs, bottom sheet, or floating buttons instead.

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer visual pace zones (easy/tempo/hard) as colored overlays rather than numeric pace thresholds
facade.label: Pace zone color bands
facade.acceptImplies: Use color-coded visual zones for pace guidance; de-emphasize numbers in favor of visual feedback loops
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize exact pace/mile splits as primary info hierarchy; color is secondary to numeric precision

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners need instant visual feedback on speed changes; a gesture-driven reveal (swipe) creates engagement vs. a static speed display
facade.label: Swipe to see pace
facade.acceptImplies: User values interactive, tactile feedback over glanceable data; motion should reward curiosity and rhythm-matching behavior
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers always-visible, scannable info; motion should be minimal and functional, not invitational

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer gentle, organic color transitions that reduce visual fatigue during outdoor runs over hard-edged, high-contrast digital interfaces
facade.label: Soft gradient background
facade.acceptImplies: User values visual comfort and natural aesthetics; app should use flowing transitions, muted palettes, and reduce stark contrast to create calm during activity
facade.rejectImplies: User confirms preference for crisp, technical visual language; design should prioritize sharp edges, bold color separation, and maximum legibility over visual softness

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
